name: qbittorrent
title: qbittorrent
summary: bittorrent client based on libtorrent-rasterbar with a Qt5 GUI
description:
bittorrent client based on libtorrent-rasterbar with a Qt5 GUI
BitTorrent client written in C++ and based on libtorrent-rasterbar with a
Qt5 user interface. qBittorrent is a feature rich but lightweight client
that is very similar to uTorrent. Its main features are:
.
* Polished uTorrent-like user interface
* Well integrated and extensible search engine
* Remote control through a Web user interface
* Vuze-compatible protocol encryption
* uTorrent-compatible Peer eXchange (PeX)
* DHT (trackerless) support
* UPnP / NAT-PMP port forwarding
* IPv6 compliant
* RSS support with advanced download filters
* Advanced control over torrent content and trackers
* IP Filtering (eMule / Peer Guardian filters)
* Torrents queueing and prioritizing
* Good localization (~25 languages supported)
* Unicode support

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use qbittorrent because bittorrent client based on libtorrent-rasterbar with a Qt5 GUI.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use qbittorrent as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `qbittorrent [--no-splash] [--webui-port=x] [TORRENT_FILE | URL]...` as documented upstream for qbittorrent.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `qbittorrent --help` as documented upstream for qbittorrent.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `qbittorrent --version` as documented upstream for qbittorrent.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair qbittorrent with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `qbittorrent --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
